text: The shipping was super fast and the product arrived in great condition. Really happy with this purchase.
notes: clear positive with single dominant theme

text: App crashes every single time I try to upload a photo larger than 5MB. Please fix this — it's been broken for weeks now.
notes: Clear bug report; action item required. Model returns 'upload' / 'photo upload' / 'app stability' depending on run — anchor uses 'upload' (substring matches 'upload', 'photo upload', 'file upload'). The earlier ['crash'] anchor was my naming preference, not a real assertion about the model's behavior.

text: The pricing tiers are confusing. The pro plan doesn't clearly explain what features are included compared to standard.
notes: complaint about clarity — negative because user is dissatisfied

text: Love the new dashboard, it's so much faster than before. One small thing — would be nice to filter by date range.
notes: positive overall but feature suggestion → action item expected

text: Great product, exceeded expectations!
notes: short positive; themes optional, no specific topic

text: Shipping was slow, customer support did not reply for three days, and the product arrived damaged. I want a refund.
notes: multi-issue complaint; multiple themes + action item

text: On Android 14, the login screen keeps redirecting back to itself in an infinite loop. Cannot sign in at all.
notes: specific bug on specific platform

text: Payment failed three times in a row even though my card has plenty of balance. The error message just says try again later.
notes: clear payment issue

text: Would love to see keyboard shortcuts for the most common actions. Would speed up my workflow significantly.
notes: feature request — neutral sentiment, action item required

text: Customer support has been unresponsive for over a week. I've sent three follow-up emails and heard nothing back so far.
notes: clear support complaint

text: Could you add a dark mode toggle? My eyes get tired using the bright interface at night.
notes: polite feature suggestion → neutral + action

text: CSV import silently fails when there are trailing commas in any row. No error message, no upload, just nothing happens.
notes: technical bug with specific repro

text: This product is not what I expected at all based on the description. I would like a full refund, please process it as soon as possible.
notes: refund demand; action required

text: The build quality is outstanding. Materials feel premium and everything is solidly constructed. Worth every penny.
notes: clear positive on quality theme; no action needed

text: product worked 1000 yrs ago and now is bad
notes: absurd premise — themes should be generic (e.g. 'quality'). Action-items presence is intentionally unasserted (null): empty list is defensible (prompt says 'don't invent'; no concrete change named in input) AND a generic non-parroting action item is also acceptable. Forbidden substrings are the load-bearing assertion: if the model DOES produce action items, none may parrot the time-travel framing.

text: absolutely love this product, best purchase I've made all year!
notes: pure praise — no action items even when the model wants to invent something. Locks in v1.1 behavior we want to keep.

text: would absolutely love a dark mode!
notes: enthusiastic feature request — should be neutral despite 'absolutely love' wording (the user is asking for a change, not praising what exists). v1.1 misclassifies as positive.

text: had a billing issue last month, support fixed it within an hour
notes: resolved complaint — positive because the resolution dominates; no action items needed since it was already fixed. Locks in v1.1 behavior.

text: the UI is gorgeous but checkout is broken and I can't pay
notes: praise + unresolved blocker — blocker dominates so negative. Forces a tie-breaking rule in the prompt.

text: 10/10 would lose all my data again, what a fantastic feature
notes: Sarcastic 'praise' wrapping a real complaint. Model often classifies positive on surface tone. Tests sentiment-vs-tone distinction beyond the enthusiastic-feature-request case.

text: Switched from a competitor product because your pricing is fairer and onboarding was much smoother
notes: Praise referencing a competitor. Themes should describe THIS product's strengths (pricing, onboarding), not extract 'competitor' as a topic.

text: Got TypeError: cannot read property 'id' of undefined when I tried to save my draft
notes: Bug report with stack-trace-flavored text. Themes should describe the feature (save, draft) not echo 'TypeError' / 'undefined' as topics.

text: Last week the app kept freezing every time I opened the dashboard
notes: Past tense but NO resolution mentioned. Distinct from resolved-past-tense (which is positive). Should stay negative — problem persists from user's perspective.

text: How does the dashboard sort feedback by date by default?
notes: Pure how-to question with no complaint clause. Tests that questions without frustration cues classify as neutral with no invented action items. Earlier version included 'Cannot find the option anywhere' — the model rightly called that negative; this version isolates the question-as-feedback pattern.

text: Just make it better. Everything about this product is frustrating right now.
notes: Vague-but-emphatic complaint with no specifics. Negative sentiment is clear; action items should NOT be invented since there's no concrete change named — counter-test to the action-item-hallucination trap.

text: Bonjour, serait-il possible d'ajouter une option pour exporter les données au format CSV? Cela m'aiderait beaucoup pour mes rapports mensuels.
notes: French polite feature request — tests fr ISO code + neutral classification on a non-English polite ask

text: El checkout no me deja pagar, keeps saying error 500. Por favor fix this asap, I have orders waiting.
notes: Code-switched Spanglish complaint — checkout theme + negative are the real assertion; language can be es or en

text: I requested account deletion two weeks ago under GDPR and have not received confirmation that my data has been removed.
notes: Compliance/legal-flavored complaint — sentiment negative, action item required, theme count bounded. Any reasonable theme name (privacy / deletion / data / account / support) is acceptable; primary assertion is that GDPR/legalese doesn't bloat themes or appear verbatim.

text: Clicking the link in your newsletter (https://example.com/promo) takes me to a 404 page. The promo already expired? Frustrating.
notes: Feedback containing a URL — themes should describe the issue (links, newsletter) not echo the URL string

text: I've been a customer for about three years now and overall I really like the service. The new analytics dashboard you shipped last quarter has been a huge improvement over the previous version, and the export to PDF works flawlessly for my team's weekly reports. That said, I want to flag two things that have become real pain points. First, the mobile app on iPad in landscape mode cuts off the right side of every chart — I have to rotate the device just to see the legend. Second, the email digest still arrives at 4am my local time despite having set the delivery preference to 9am six months ago. Please look into both.
notes: Long multi-paragraph feedback with praise + unresolved blockers — tests that verbose input stays within theme cap and blockers dominate sentiment

text: Customer service was responsive but the support staff couldn't actually resolve my issue, and the service team kept transferring me around.
notes: Multiple synonyms for one concept (customer service / support staff / service team) — tests v1.2 canonicalization rule

text: Es wäre toll, wenn Sie eine Dunkelmodus-Option für die Benutzeroberfläche hinzufügen könnten. Vielen Dank!
notes: German polite feature request — tests de ISO code + neutral on enthusiastic-but-asking phrasing

text: I am writing to formally raise a concern regarding the recurring delays in processing my subscription renewal. This has occurred on three separate occasions and each time I have had to contact your team to have the issue resolved manually. The experience is not acceptable for a paid customer.
notes: Red-team: formal/polite register may trick model toward neutral. The case's real assertion is sentiment=negative under formal phrasing (no exclamation marks, no slang) — the model's theme choice can reasonably be 'subscription renewal' / 'billing' / 'support' / 'renewal', so subset is intentionally open.

text: The interface is beautiful, the documentation is excellent, the onboarding is smooth, and the API is well-designed. The only thing is that data exports are completely broken and I cannot get my data out at all.
notes: Red-team: 4:1 praise-to-blocker ratio. Prompt says blocker dominates, but the sheer volume of positive sentences may push the model to positive or neutral. Tests whether the prompt's tie-breaker holds under weight-of-evidence pressure.

text: Your team fixed the login bug I reported last month, which I really appreciated. But it came back again this week — I still can't sign in from Safari on iOS.
notes: Red-team: resolved-then-recurred pattern. The prompt's resolved-past-tense rule makes 'resolved dominates' → positive, but this case has a NEW unresolved blocker. Model may incorrectly fire the resolution heuristic on the phrase 'fixed' and classify positive.

text: The search results load in about four seconds on my connection. I have a standard broadband plan so that might be normal.
notes: Red-team: latent complaint framed as observation with self-doubt ('might be normal'). Model will likely invent 'improve search performance' even though the user is NOT requesting a fix. Tests action-item grounding on hedged observations.

text: I would genuinely really love it if the reports section could show year-over-year comparisons. It would make a world of difference for my quarterly reviews.
notes: Red-team: stacked intensifiers ('genuinely really love it') wrapped around a feature request. The prompt explicitly says enthusiastic tone does NOT make a pure request positive, but 'genuinely really love' is a stronger signal than 'would absolutely love' (the existing golden). Tests whether the rule degrades under heavier sentiment loading. Subset uses 'report' so it substring-matches both 'reports' and 'reporting'.

text: The billing statement is impossible to read. The numbers are crammed together with no spacing and the column headers are cut off.
notes: Red-team: single-topic feedback (billing readability) but the model may split it into 'billing' + 'ui' + 'design' over-extracting. Asserts max_count=1 to catch over-extraction on genuinely single-topic complaints.

text: We integrated this into our onboarding pipeline about six months ago. We currently process around two hundred items per day through the batch endpoint.
notes: Red-team: pure descriptive/informational statement — no praise, no complaint, no request. The prompt's tie-breaker only covers three branches (keep doing / fix / add new thing). This falls through all three; model may invent themes or assign positive due to implicit satisfaction.

text: The search bar autocomplete is way too slow, the suggestions it shows are completely irrelevant to what I type, and the dropdown doesn't even close properly after I pick something. Basically the whole search experience is broken.
notes: One-topic discipline on a non-billing subject. All three complaints (slow autocomplete, bad suggestions, broken dropdown) are sub-aspects of the search feature. Asserts max_count=1.

text: The new batch export feature is excellent. It handles files of all sizes, the progress bar is accurate, and the downloaded file opens cleanly in Excel every time. Nothing to complain about.
notes: Detailed praise of a specific feature with zero complaint or request. Tests that grounded action-item rule produces an empty list when nothing is implied.

text: Really appreciate how the system logs me out automatically every 20 minutes. Makes staying productive an absolute joy when I'm in the middle of filling out a long form.
notes: Understated sarcasm without obvious tells (no '10/10', no exclamation marks). Tests that pattern-matching on surface praise words ('really appreciate', 'absolute joy') doesn't flip sentiment to positive.

text: The getting-started guide is confusing, the initial setup wizard skips too many steps, and the welcome tutorial doesn't explain the core workflow at all. New users will be completely lost.
notes: Three onboarding-cluster synonyms ('getting-started guide', 'setup wizard', 'welcome tutorial') must collapse to a single theme. Tests v1.5 onboarding synonym family rule.

text: ダッシュボードの読み込みが非常に遅いです。毎回10秒以上かかっており、業務に支障が出ています。改善をお願いします。
notes: Japanese-only feedback (CJK script). Tests ISO 639-1 'ja' detection and correct sentiment/action extraction on non-Latin script. First non-Latin-script case in the suite.

text: Does the API support webhook retries when the endpoint returns a 5xx? And if so, is there a way to configure the retry interval? We are evaluating whether this fits our reliability requirements.
notes: Multi-sentence technical question with no frustration cue and no implicit feature request — user is evaluating, not demanding. Tests that questions-as-evaluation don't trigger action items.

text: You've done an incredible job with the new reporting module — it's genuinely one of the best data views I've used. Now I'd love it if you could add a way to schedule automated report delivery via email.
notes: Substantial opening praise + closing feature request. Tests v1.5's praise-wrapping-request tie-breaker — the request defines dominant intent, sentiment is neutral despite heavy praise.

text: THIS IS ABSOLUTELY UNACCEPTABLE. MY ACCOUNT HAS BEEN LOCKED FOR THREE DAYS AND NOBODY FROM SUPPORT HAS RESPONDED TO ANY OF MY EMAILS. FIX THIS NOW.
notes: ALL-CAPS rage with two distinct subjects (account lockout + support silence). Tests that uppercase screaming doesn't push the model to invent extra themes or suppress action items.

text: Loving the app so far ❤️ The dashboard is super clean and the charts load instantly ⚡. Only wish: please add the ability to pin my top 3 reports so I don't have to scroll every time 🙏
notes: Emoji-heavy positive feedback with one specific feature request (pin reports). Tests v1.5's actions-independent-of-sentiment rule — positive sentiment plus extracted action item.

text: التطبيق يتعطل باستمرار عند محاولة فتح الإعدادات. هذا مزعج جداً وأحتاج إلى حل سريع.
notes: Arabic (RTL script) crash/settings complaint. Primary assertion is language=ar; tests detection on a script family with no examples in the prompt.

text: Hola, tengo un problema con el dashboard. Cuando intento ver los analytics de la semana pasada, la página se queda cargando para siempre y nunca carga los datos.
notes: Mostly Spanish with two English product nouns ('dashboard', 'analytics'). Tests that isolated English brand words don't flip language detection to en.

text: We keep hitting your rate limit — getting 429s constantly. Our quota runs out within the first hour of every day and then the whole pipeline is throttled for the rest of the day.
notes: Developer vocabulary: 'rate limit', '429', 'quota', 'throttled' all describe one API constraint. Tests v1.5's rate-limit synonym family rule.

text: Works as expected. No issues so far.
notes: Minimalist neutral informational — flat acknowledgment with no praise, complaint, or request. Tests v1.5's flat-acknowledgment-is-neutral branch.

text: Why does the export button even exist if clicking it never actually exports anything? I've tried five times this week.
notes: Rhetorical question framing a real bug ('five times this week'). Tests that question syntax doesn't suppress action items the way a genuine evaluation question would.
